profile.title: Study Program Profile
profile.subtitle: About Us
profile.history: The Development Economics Study Program at the Faculty of Economics and Business, Universitas Pasundan (UNPAS), was established in 1974. Over more than five decades, the program has made tangible contributions in producing quality human resources in the field of development economics. Located in Bandung, West Java, UNPAS is committed to producing graduates who can compete at both national and international levels.
profile.vision: To become a distinguished, innovative, and internationally competitive Development Economics Study Program in sustainable development economics based on Islamic values by 2030.
missions: To provide quality, relevant, and adaptive development economics education in response to advances in science and technology.
To conduct innovative scientific research in development economics to contribute to knowledge and national development.
To implement community service programs that contribute to improving social welfare.
To develop strategic cooperation with various domestic and international institutions to improve academic quality and professional networks.
To create a conducive academic environment with integrity, based on Islamic values in campus life.
objectives: To produce competent, professional, and ethical graduates in the field of development economics.
To produce nationally and internationally recognized research and scientific publications.
To make real contributions to society through sustainable community service programs.
To build strategic partnerships to improve graduate quality and program relevance.

achievements.title: Accreditation B – BAN-PT
achievements.desc: The study program received a B accreditation from the National Accreditation Board for Higher Education.

achievements.title: National PKM Champion
achievements.desc: EP UNPAS students won the national Student Creativity Program in the research category.

achievements.title: International MoU
achievements.desc: Signing of international cooperation agreements with universities in Malaysia and Thailand.

academics.title: Academic Programs
academics.subtitle: Curriculum & Programs
academics.program_desc: The Bachelor's Program in Development Economics is designed to equip students with comprehensive theoretical knowledge and analytical skills in the field of development economics.

concentrations.name: Monetary Economics & Banking
concentrations.desc: Studying monetary policy, banking systems, and macroeconomic financial management.

concentrations.name: Regional Planning & Development
concentrations.desc: Focused on regional development planning, regional autonomy, and public policy.

concentrations.name: Resource & Environmental Economics
concentrations.desc: Examining natural resource management and environmental policy in sustainable development.

prospects.title: Government Agencies
prospects.desc: Ministry of Finance, Bappenas, Bank Indonesia, BPS, and regional agencies.

prospects.title: Financial Institutions
prospects.desc: Banking, OJK, state-owned financial enterprises, and financing institutions.

prospects.title: Economic Consultants
prospects.desc: Development consultants, policy analysts, and economic researchers.

prospects.title: Academics & Researchers
prospects.desc: Lecturers, researchers at national and international research institutions.
